verdicts.explanation: The agent booked X1+Y1 before confirming their pair quote, and when the latest quote came back at $1030 (over the $1000 cap) it filed the expense report and sent the itinerary anyway instead of remediating.
verdicts.culprit: Steps 7-15: booking preceded the required latest-quote confirmation, and the $1030 live quote at step 12 violated the <=$1000 rule yet the agent proceeded to file and send.

verdicts.explanation: The agent booked X1+Y1 before confirming a quote for the pair, and when the latest quote (step 12) came back at $1030 — exceeding the $1000 cap — it still filed the expense report and sent the itinerary instead of correcting the booking.
verdicts.culprit: Budget-cap violation: latest_quote at step 12 showed X1+Y1 = $1030 > $1000, yet the agent proceeded (steps 13-16); it also booked (steps 7-9) before confirming the quote for the exact pair.

verdicts.explanation: The agent booked flight X1 and hotel Y1 without first confirming a latest quote for the pair (its step-5 quote call was malformed and errored), and when the post-booking quote came back at $1030 — exceeding the $1000 cap — it ignored the violation and proceeded to file the expense report and send the itinerary.
verdicts.culprit: Agent decision: after the latest_quote error at step 6 (it passed only the flight, no hotel), the agent skipped re-quoting and booked X1+Y1 unverified, violating the "confirm the latest quote for the exact pair you book" rule; the authoritative live quote was $1030 > $1000, and the agent compounded the error by filing and sending anyway instead of rebooking a compliant pair.

verdicts.explanation: The agent booked X1+Y1 without a confirmed quote (its pre-booking latest_quote calls errored for lack of a hotel and it just repeated them), and when the post-booking quote came back at $1030 — over the $1000 latest-quote cap — it still filed the expense report and sent the itinerary instead of correcting the booking.
verdicts.culprit: Budget/verification violation at steps 9-18: booking proceeded without a valid latest quote for the exact pair, and the confirmed quote v1 X1+Y1=$1030 exceeded the $1000 policy cap, yet the agent completed expense filing and itinerary send anyway.

verdicts.explanation: The agent booked X1+Y1 before obtaining a valid quote (its two pre-booking latest_quote calls errored with a missing hotel), and when the post-booking quote came back at $1030 — exceeding the $1000 latest-quote cap — it still filed the expense report and sent the itinerary instead of remediating.
verdicts.culprit: Budget violation at steps 13-18: latest quote for the booked pair X1+Y1 was $1030 (> $1000 cap), yet the agent proceeded to file the expense report and send the itinerary; compounded by booking (steps 9-12) before confirming the quote for the exact pair.

verdicts.explanation: The agent booked the X1+Y1 pair without ever obtaining a valid pre-booking quote — its two latest_quote calls (steps 5 and 7) omitted the required 'hotel' argument and errored, and instead of fixing the call it proceeded to book (steps 9-11); the quote confirmed only after booking (step 13) came back $1030, violating the <= $1000 latest-quote cap, and the agent still filed the expense report and sent the itinerary despite the over-budget total.
verdicts.culprit: Agent decision: after two malformed latest_quote calls (passing only flight X1, no hotel) failed with errors, the agent abandoned quote confirmation and booked flight X1 + hotel Y1 unverified, breaching the "confirm the latest quote for the exact pair you book" rule; the live quote turned out to be $1030 > $1000, and the agent proceeded to file/send anyway instead of rebooking a compliant pair.
